steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.Dominum: 'Dominum' is a noun meaning 'the Lord' in the accusative
hints.Deum: 'Deum' is a noun meaning 'God' in the accusative

hints.to speak to: 'to speak to' is 'alloqui' — 'adorabis' comes from 'adorare' (to worship)

hints.present indicative: Present 2nd person would be 'adoras' — 'adorabis' with -bis is future

hints.orno, ornare, ornavi, ornatum: These are principal parts of 'orno' (to decorate/equip)
hints.oro, orare, oravi, oratum: These are principal parts of 'oro' (to pray/speak)
hints.amo, amare, amavi, amatum: These are principal parts of 'amo' (to love)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'Dominus' — 'Dominum' with -um is accusative
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'Domino' — 'Dominum' is accusative, direct object

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'Domini' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'Domini' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'Domini' ends in -i

hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'Deo' — 'Deum' is accusative, in apposition with 'Dominum'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'Dei' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'Dei' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'Dei' ends in -i

translation: You shall worship the Lord your God
